evals.prompt: Build a new route for a game detail page at src/routes/$username.$gameId.tsx. It should show: the game result, date played, both players' ratings, the opening name, and a placeholder for the move list (just a card with 'Move analysis coming soon'). Use real data shapes from the existing codebase where possible.
evals.expected_output: A TSX file that follows the design language: text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight for the page title, text-sm for body content, text-muted-foreground for metadata, shadcn Table or Card components, no raw Tailwind colors, skeleton loading state, and an empty state. No font-bold, no shadow-md, no decorative elements.

assertions.text: No raw Tailwind color utilities used (no bg-blue-*, text-gray-*, text-green-*, bg-red-*, etc.)
Page title includes text-2xl and tracking-tight
Loading state uses Skeleton component (not a spinner or null)
No shadow-md or heavier shadow class used

evals.prompt: Add a stats summary row above the games table in src/routes/$username.tsx that shows total games, wins, losses, and draws as four numbers side by side. It should pull from the existing gamesData query result.
evals.expected_output: A stat summary component using the correct typography scale (text-2xl or text-lg for numbers, text-sm text-muted-foreground for labels), consistent gap-6 or gap-8 between stats, semantic colors (green for wins, destructive for losses, muted for draws), no cards wrapping individual stats unless it's a proper grid of all four, no shadows.

assertions.text: No raw Tailwind color utilities used for win/loss/draw (no text-green-*, text-red-*, bg-green-*, etc.)
Win count uses a semantic success/green token (e.g. chart-2 CSS var or text-[hsl(var(--chart-2))]) not a hardcoded Tailwind green
Loss count uses destructive color token (text-destructive or similar)
Consistent spacing between stat items (gap-6 or gap-8)

evals.prompt: Review the dashboard page at src/routes/$username.tsx for any violations of the project's design language and list what should be fixed. Then fix them.
evals.expected_output: Identifies and fixes real violations: font-bold on the h1 (should be font-semibold), table headers lack the uppercase tracking-wide muted style. Should NOT invent violations that don't exist or over-engineer correct patterns.

assertions.text: Identifies the font-bold class on the h1 player name as a violation
Identifies table headers missing text-xs text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide as a violation
Fixed file replaces font-bold with font-semibold on the page title/h1
Fixed file updates TableHead elements to use subtle muted styling
Does not invent violations that don't exist
